cloudflare_exposure_incident.cause: The custom domain Access application did not cover the default Pages production hostname or immutable preview hostnames, which Cloudflare documents as public by default.
cloudflare_exposure_incident.exposed_artifact_class: redacted static Memory Atlas publish artifact; no raw database, transcript, cookie, session, token, key, or credential artifact
immediate_actions: Replaced production content with a fail-closed placeholder and explicit safe-marker memory_atlas.json.
Deleted the two real-artifact deployments 501108af and 86e82465.
Verified known direct URLs no longer returned real Memory Atlas fields.
cloudflare_exposure_incident.permanent_fix: Added separate owner-allowlist Access applications for openai-memory-atlas.pages.dev and *.openai-memory-atlas.pages.dev while retaining the custom-domain application.
cloudflare_exposure_incident.final_verification: Custom, production Pages, and preview Pages root/JSON paths all return an unauthenticated Access challenge; an allowed Chrome identity loads the current app and fetches memory_atlas.json.

cloudflare_fail_closed_mitigation_histor.reason: Cloudflare Access was previously not enabled, so the Pages project and custom-domain binding were removed to prevent public exposure.
cloudflare_fail_closed_mitigation_histor.resolution: Access is enabled, all three required hostname classes are protected, and the clean real deployment has independently verified challenge and allowed-user evidence.
